event: Peter II Duke of Bourbon presented by Saint Peter (donor panel)
symbols: keys held by Saint Peter — the keys of the kingdom given by Christ (Matthew 16:19)
saint's hand on the donor's shoulder — patronal protection, the saint presenting the faithful to God
donor's praying hands — the posture of devotion and supplication before God
green mantle — the verdant life of the saint who guards the gates of heaven
description: A half-length painting showing two figures before an arched window revealing a green landscape with a river. In the foreground, a man with dark hair in a black garment kneels with hands clasped in prayer. Behind and above him, a larger bearded figure in a green mantle holds a set of golden keys in one hand while the other hand rests protectively on the kneeling man's shoulder. The bearded saint gazes outward with a dignified expression. The landscape behind shows gentle hills, trees, and a winding river.
theological_description: This donor panel from a triptych shows Peter II Duke of Bourbon under the protection of his patron saint, the Apostle Peter. The keys in Peter's hand are his defining attribute, given by Christ himself: 'I will give you the keys of the kingdom of heaven; whatever you bind on earth will be bound in heaven' (Matthew 16:18-19). The saint's protective hand on the donor's shoulder enacts the intercessory role of the saints — presenting the faithful before God. This tradition draws on the Pauline understanding that the Church is one body where each member cares for the others (1 Corinthians 12:25-26). The donor's praying hands express the humility necessary for approaching God: 'Humble yourselves before the Lord, and he will lift you up' (James 4:10).

scripture_refs.reason: Christ giving Peter the keys of the kingdom — the golden keys held by the saint
Humble yourselves before the Lord — the donor's posture of humble prayer
Members of the body caring for one another — the saint's intercessory patronage
